evals.prompt: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation to this Flask app based on its inventory. The service is at examples/python/flask-basic/
evals.expected_output: OTel SDK installed, auto-instrumentation configured, custom metrics added, inventory Status columns updated to OK
expectations: An otel_setup.py or similar OTel initialization file is created
opentelemetry packages are added to pyproject.toml or requirements.txt
Auto-instrumentation for Flask HTTP is configured
At least one custom metric (Counter, Histogram, or Gauge) is added in application code
inventory.md Status column is updated to OK for instrumented signals

evals.prompt: Instrument this Go key-value store service based on the observability inventory. The code is at examples/go/kvstore/
evals.expected_output: OTel SDK initialized, otelhttp middleware added, custom spans and metrics for store operations
expectations: go.opentelemetry.io dependencies are added to go.mod
An OTel SDK initialization function is created or added to main
otelhttp or otelchi middleware is wired into the HTTP server
Custom spans are added for store operations (get, set, delete, or search)
At least one custom metric instrument is created (Counter, Histogram, or Gauge)
inventory.md Status column is updated to OK for instrumented signals
